quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "Gadget leads with $2,392.00 in revenue, about 33.4% of the total" and "West trails at $1,414.82 (19.8%)."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names products/regions with figures, e.g. "Prioritize Gadget inventory and promotions, since it generates one-third of all revenue" and "Investigate why West underperforms."

insight_density.why: The section prose mostly restates table totals and rankings, with the concentration point ("Gadget...one-third of all revenue") being the main real, actionable observation.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. 'Gadget leads with $2,392.00 in revenue, about 33.4% of the total' and each region shown with dollars and percentage.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names concrete products, regions, and figures ('Prioritize Gadget inventory...one-third of all revenue', 'Investigate why West underperforms', 'Push higher-priced items like Gadget, as median order value sits near $205').

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable observations beyond table restatement—Gadget's revenue concentration at one-third, West as a lagging outlier at 19.8%, and the ~$205 median as a pricing lever.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — product shares ($2,392.00, 33.4%; 29.6%; 21.1%; 15.8%) and regional shares (30.4%, 25.6%, 24.2%, 19.8%).

specificity.why: Each recommendation names concrete products/regions and figures ('Prioritize Gadget inventory... one-third of all revenue', 'Investigate why West underperforms', 'median order value sits near $205').

insight_density.why: It flags concentration (Gadget at one-third) and the West outlier, but the rest of the prose mostly restates the totals and percentages the tables already imply rather than surfacing multiple non-obvious observations.
